zh.Conington, Huntingdonshire, England: 英國亨廷格多史爾康寧頓
zh.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Conington是Bernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and Exton和Agnes的兒子，於1317年6月13日出生在英國亨廷格多史爾康寧頓。他於大約1335年與Margaret de Hardreshull結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和9個女兒的父母。他去世於1346年5月10日之前在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Conington, Huntingdonshire, England: 英国亨廷格多史爾康寧頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Conington是Bernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and Exton和Agnes的儿子，于1317年6月13日出生在英国亨廷格多史爾康寧頓。他于大约1335年与Margaret de Hardreshull结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和9个女儿的父母。他去世于1346年5月10日之前在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Polish, Slovenian, Czech, Sorbian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from Slavic brus ‘whetstone’, presumably a metonymic occupational name for someone who honed or sharpened blades; possibly also a nickname for a clumsy or coarse fellow. Compare Bruss .
Slovenian: from an old dialect short form of the personal name Ambrož (see Ambrose ). Compare Bruss .
French: topographic name for someone living on a heath, from Occitan brusc ‘heather’, or a habitational name from (Le/Les) Brus, the name of several places in southern France.

variants.name: Bruss
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Eldor, Reinhard, Rudie, Wilhelm.
German (also Brüss): from a short form of the medieval personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).
Americanized or Germanized form of Polish, Slovenian, Czech, Sorbian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic)   Brus  , meaning ‘whetstone’ and, as a Slovenian name, also ‘Ambrose’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Conington was born on 13 June 1317, in Conington, Huntingdonshire, England as the son of Bernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and Exton and Agnes. He married Margaret de Hardreshull about 1335. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 9 daughters. He died before 10 May 1346, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Conington est né le 13 juin 1317, à Conington, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, fils de Bernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and Exton et Agnes. Il a épousé Margaret de Hardreshull environ 1335. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 9 filles. Il est décédé avant 10 mai 1346, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Conington wurde am 13. Juni 1317 in Conington, Huntingdonshire, England, als Sohn von Bernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and Exton und Agnes geboren. Er hat um 1335 Margaret de Hardreshull geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 9 Töchter. Er starb vor 10. Mai 1346 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Conington è nato il il 13 giugno 1317 nel Conington, Huntingdonshire, England come figlio di Bernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and Exton e Agnes. Lui sposato Margaret de Hardreshull circa 1335. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 9 figlie. Morì prima del 10 maggio 1346, nel sua cittadina.

ja.Conington, Huntingdonshire, England: イギリスハンティングドンシュアイアコニントン
ja.Exton, Rutland, England: イギリスルトランドエクストン
ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Coningtonは1317年6月13日にイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアコニントンでBernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and ExtonとAgnesの息子として生まれた。約1335年にMargaret de Hardreshullと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼は1346年5月10日以前に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Conington(은)는 1317년 6월 13일 영국 헌틴그도실르 코닝턴에서 Bernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and Exton(와)과 Agnes의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1335년 Margaret de Hardreshull(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1346년 5월 10일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.13 June 1317–before 10 May 1346: 13 de junho de 1317–antes 10 de maio de 1346
pt.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Conington nasceu em 13 de junho de 1317, em Conington, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra filho de Bernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and Exton e Agnes. Ele casou-se com Margaret de Hardreshull aproximadamente 1335. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 9 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 10 de maio de 1346, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brus Lord of Conington родился 13 июня 1317 г. в местности конингтон, хантингдоншир, Англия. Его родителей звали Bernard Bruce Lord of Conningoton and Exton и Agnes. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Hardreshull около 1335 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 9 дочерей. Он умер перед 10 май 1346 г. в его родном городе.

Se casó con Margaret de Hardreshull aproximadamente 1335. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 9 hijas. Murió antes de 10 de mayo de 1346, en su pueblo.
